skill.name: email-marketing-bible
skill.description: A data-backed email marketing reference for audits, automation flows, deliverability, copywriting, segmentation, compliance, platform selection, and industry benchmarks.
skill.summary: Audit email programs, plan lifecycle flows, and draft campaigns using benchmark-backed guidance.
skill.author: CosmoBlk

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many high and medium patterns, but review shows they are markdown examples, inline formatting, benchmark text, and reference links rather than executable code. The only confirmed concern is a user-run install command that clones a community repository into a hidden Claude skills directory.

low_findings.title: Community Skill Install Writes Into Hidden Claude Directory
low_findings.description: README.md includes a user-run git clone command that installs the community repository under ~/.claude/skills. This is expected for a Claude skill, but users should trust the source before installing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and hidden directory path are directly present. It is not auto-executed by the skill, so the risk is limited to user-initiated installation.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The alleged Ruby or shell backtick execution appears in fenced prompts, inline marketing labels, CSS syntax, and DNS examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command substitution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are markdown documentation or email authentication examples. There is no runnable source file or dynamic execution path.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are documentation links to the author, the repository, and chapter pages. The skill does not contain code that performs HTTP requests or sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible markdown references. No evidence found of fetch, curl, webhook posting, credential handling, or covert network behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Content False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported lines discuss marketing strategy, design, copywriting frameworks, or standard email authentication. No weak cryptographic algorithm use or implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found prose or markdown section text. The only crypto-related content recommends DKIM and DMARC, not weak crypto.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Marketing Prose
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain ROI and newsletter business guidance. No commands, APIs, or instructions collect host, user, network, or process information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is ordinary email marketing text. No evidence found of OS reconnaissance or environment inspection.

content.value_statement: Email teams often lack clear benchmarks and tested lifecycle patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured email marketing guidance.
actual_capabilities: Reviews email programs for missing flows, weak segments, and deliverability gaps.
Drafts lifecycle emails with subject lines, preview text, body copy, and calls to action.
Designs welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, win-back, sunset, and nurture sequences.
Provides benchmarks for open rates, click rates, unsubscribe rates, and revenue per email.
Compares email platforms by use case, budget, list size, and operating model.
Summarizes compliance considerations for GDPR, CAN-SPAM, CASL, CCPA, and Spam Act rules.
limitations: It is a static knowledge base and does not connect to email platforms by itself.
Benchmarks may differ from current platform data or a specific audience.
Compliance guidance is informational and is not legal advice.
Generated copy still needs brand, offer, and factual review before sending.

use_cases.title: Audit a Revenue Email Program
use_cases.description: Find missing lifecycle flows, segment problems, and deliverability risks in an existing email setup.

use_cases.title: Plan a SaaS Onboarding Sequence
use_cases.description: Create behavior-based emails that guide activation, retention, expansion, and re-engagement.

use_cases.title: Build a Cold Outreach Framework
use_cases.description: Draft compliant outbound sequences with infrastructure separation, warming guidance, and concise copy.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Email Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for a small team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current email setup. I use [platform], sell [product], and send [frequency]. What should I fix first?

prompt_templates.title: Create a Lifecycle Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a [flow type] sequence for [business type]. Include timing, trigger rules, email goals, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Deliverability Problems
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose why my open rate fell from [old rate] to [new rate]. Review authentication, reputation, content, and sending patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Complete Email Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across channels and lifecycle stages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day email strategy for [company]. Include segmentation, flows, campaigns, tests, compliance checks, and measurement.

output_examples.input: Audit a Shopify skincare brand using Klaviyo with welcome, cart, and weekly newsletter emails.
output: Priority fixes: add post-purchase, browse abandonment, sunset, and replenishment flows.
Segment by recent clicks, first purchase status, skin concern, and predicted reorder window.
Track revenue per recipient, click rate, complaint rate, and repeat purchase rate.

output_examples.input: Create a win-back sequence for subscribers inactive for 90 days.
output: Email 1: remind subscribers of the core value and ask one preference question.
Email 2: share a strong offer or best content based on past behavior.
Email 3: confirm reduced frequency or suppression if there is no response.

output_examples.input: Compare Mailchimp, Klaviyo, and ActiveCampaign for a 7,000 subscriber ecommerce list.
output: Klaviyo fits ecommerce data depth and revenue reporting best.
Mailchimp can work for simpler campaigns with lighter automation needs.
ActiveCampaign is strongest when CRM-style automation matters more than product data.

best_practices: Provide business model, list size, platform, revenue, and current flow details.
Ask for benchmarks and measurement plans alongside creative recommendations.
Review generated compliance guidance with local counsel before major sending changes.
anti_patterns: Do not send generated copy without brand, product, and legal review.
Do not optimize only for open rates after Apple Mail Privacy Protection.
Do not use cold email guidance to contact people without checking local consent rules.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails or connect to my ESP?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, audits, drafts, and plans. You must implement changes in your email platform.

faq.question: Which email platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It references platforms such as Klaviyo, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, HubSpot, Kit, Brevo, beehiiv, Omnisend, and Postmark.

faq.question: Can it help with deliverability issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes authentication, sender reputation, warming, list hygiene, complaint rates, and remediation guidance.

faq.question: Is the compliance guidance legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes common email rules, but legal review is required for jurisdiction-specific decisions.

faq.question: Can it write email copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft lifecycle, promotional, newsletter, and cold emails using documented copywriting frameworks.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Growth teams, ecommerce operators, newsletter publishers, agencies, SaaS marketers, and sales teams can use it.
